trades.comments: Changed my mind on this, will do either of the offers I've sent out...
Need to include Clark in order to make the salary cap work.
I want to hold on to my 2013 picks so I am re-countering with the trade parameters we discussed in e-mail :-)
Here is what I would rather do.  BTW - not interested in Cecil Short
We both have K's on same bye weeks.  Swap our #2 K's?
I do not any salary cap room to speak of so I cannot take on more cap than what I give up.  How about this as all have only 1 year contracts?
Want to trade first round picks so that you have a Draft pick this year? :-)
Dan - looks like no "conditions" - let me know if this works
Oh i know i will regret this at some point.. Taylor will become the next Julio Jones.... but need to get strong for this final push

I get where you are coming from in your counter proposal.  If I move Mahomes now I am basically playing for next season and so I am in the mindset of acquiring draft capital.  If I am going to lose a 2nd, I would like to get the 3rd then as well. Also, I would also be interested in the trade without giving up my 2nd and not acquiring your 3rd.  Let me know.
Per your message I threw in a draft pick for the trade.
Players included to make the salary cap work.

Not sure if you'd be interested, but your kickers have the same bye week.
This trade would give you a 3rd round pick, vastly improve your defense, and would also allow you to not take a hit on your future salary cap on cut down day.
I could do this one.
I'll have to drop Blount if you plan to accept, so let me know beforehand please.
I threw in next years 3rd. I want to keep my 2.17... besides there is only 2 spots between 2.17 and your 3rd rounder.... Not much of a difference and you pick up a 3rd next year.

This is the only trade I could give you for Welker that would make the cap space work out and still include DeAndre Hopkins. If you're interested, we would need to break this trade into smaller chunks so you can drop my scraps without hitting your roster limit (plus I'll have to move some of my guys off the practice squad so I don't go under the minimum). If this works for you, let me know and we'll coordinate with Brandon.
I do not any salary cap room to speak of so I cannot take on more cap than what I give up.  How about this as all have only 1 year contracts?
This would help you with your cap space next season.
I'm sure your Rd 3 next year will be at the end of the Rd, so let's bump my spot back one.  It's my way of playing hardball!  LOL

I'm an idiot. I was thinking Brandon already processed.

Split the difference

trades.comments: How about this....
I believe this would fit under the salary cap.
I could do this one.
Not as big on the Jets defense, especially against Cincy. If we could get Prater added in I'd send the pick. Noticed you have 2 years on him, so I understand if you'd rather keep him.

This is the only trade I could give you for Welker that would make the cap space work out and still include DeAndre Hopkins. If you're interested, we would need to break this trade into smaller chunks so you can drop my scraps without hitting your roster limit (plus I'll have to move some of my guys off the practice squad so I don't go under the minimum). If this works for you, let me know and we'll coordinate with Brandon.
This would help you with your cap space next season.

Very fair and I completely understand, but I think Funchess has a small chance for a break out season. I'd do this (take out Funchess due to his high ceiling on GB) and replace with Mitchell.  You get your $900K cap relief immediately which will help with your rookies to be drafted and free agent pickups. having Dissly and Olsen on the same team is useless to us unless one gets hurt.
I'll take 1 of those guys
I'd be willing to make that trade straight up with no draft picks, but otherwise I'd rather stand pat with Dotson on a 4-year than DK on a 1-year.

I think this would work. It gives me the 2nd rounder I'm looking for. I end up shouldering a couple of your overvalued contracts and will have to take the cap hit when I cut Dissly, but it's less than cutting Diggs.
Players included to make the salary cap work.
Offering in case you'd like to shore up your TE roster ahead of the playoffs.

draftPicks.comments: Was hoping Kendricks would fall on more pick so I could grab him.
The ultimate low-floor, high-ceiling pick.
Vit, you traded for an expensive Vick contract then draft Griffin instead of a WR, and you expect me to take you seriously?
Damn, was really expecting Fleener to be there at 9.

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List At this point we're just taking shots in the dark at RB, but I admit I was hoping Polk would fall to me. Nice snag, Wascawy. (Comments Added Tue May 8 9:57:48 p.m. PT 2012)

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List I was surprised that Patterson slipped so far, and was really hoping he'd keep dropping. That said, DeAndre Hopkins seems ready to drop right into the Houston offense. (Comments Added Mon May 6 4:23:56 p.m. PT 2013)
Pick made based on Pre-Draft List Hoping he turns into my long-term backup for Andrew Luck so I don't have to play QB roulette on my bench. (Comments Added Mon May 6 4:25:36 p.m. PT 2013)

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List I had Davis, Taylor, and Swope on my list in that order for the last three picks, so on my board I came in 2nd out of 3 over the last three picks. That said, Taylor is only on my list because I already have Ryan Williams, with whom Taylor will be competing for the 2nd string job. (Comments Added Sat May 11 7:15:13 p.m. PT 2013)
I was camping, had no internet, and hadn't planned my draft, so this pick was a total shot from the hip. That said, I'm pretty happy with this pick. (Comments Added Sun May 18 6:46:03 p.m. PT 2014)
Pick made based on Pre-Draft List Shoring up my depth at TE. (Comments Added Mon May 19 5:04:42 p.m. PT 2014)

Mister Irrelevant: McKinnon was slated to be a decent fantasy rookie prospect until he was drafted by the Vikings and destined to ride the bench behind Purple Jesus. He's the perfect long-term gamble for the last pick in the draft. (Comments Added Wed May 21 9:53:51 p.m. PT 2014)

draftPicks.comments: Two Browns on my roster now...  This'll probably be the first season I ever consider watching a Browns game over another game on Sundays!  Welcome to the Wascawy Wabbits TRich!

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List I was hoping Martin would somehow fall to the #6 spot since I'm a bit light at the RB spot...  Thought about Wilson here, but his severe case of fumble-itis scared me off.  Instead I'll go with the guy who's 6'3, ran a 4.4 at the combine, and is going to be Larry Fitzgerald's understudy!   (Comments Added Sun May 6 5:51:11 a.m. PT 2012)

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List Polk's fall ends here..  Prior to the draft he was graded as a top 5 RB..  Low risk picking him at this point of the draft.  Had interest in Wylie, but was taken 2 picks earlier (Comments Added Tue May 8 9:15:19 p.m. PT 2012)

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List Probably won't be a lot of use this season, but could easily be the RB2 on the Chiefs next season which is a valuable spot to be in. (Comments Added Tue May 8 9:24:46 p.m. PT 2012)
Sorry about that guys. Away this weekend as well as fielding offers. Going with Eifert here. Missing a top TE on my team which I hope he can be
Gonna swing for the fence with this pick and go Latavius. Possibly the starting RB next season?

Pick made based on Pre-Draft List Boyce and Charles Johnson were the 2 guys I was really hoping to see slide to this pick, but I'm happy to see Harper join my team. Hopefully he'll carve out a possession type role with the Seahawks and build chemistry with Russ Wilson.  (Comments Added Fri May 10 10:08:15 p.m. PT 2013)

Going for a boom pick here. Starter next year in Cleveland or outta the league?

trades.comments: Does this work?   Think there is alot of talent at 1.03 this year and Bortles is solid for the next 3

Hi Mike. Are you planning on tagging both Mark Ingram and Lamar Miller? If you don't plan on offering one of them a tag, I would trade your 2nd round pick for the chance to tag one of them. If you are tagging both, I would also be interested in Carlos Hyde for the pick. Let me know what you think.
Could you consider trading AJ Brown? He is on a one year contract and you would have Mike Williams available next year along with a first round pick.

trades.comments: Very fair and I completely understand, but I think Funchess has a small chance for a break out season. I'd do this (take out Funchess due to his high ceiling on GB) and replace with Mitchell.  You get your $900K cap relief immediately which will help with your rookies to be drafted and free agent pickups. having Dissly and Olsen on the same team is useless to us unless one gets hurt.
I'm sure your Rd 3 next year will be at the end of the Rd, so let's bump my spot back one.  It's my way of playing hardball!  LOL
I'm an idiot. I was thinking Brandon already processed.

I get where you are coming from in your counter proposal.  If I move Mahomes now I am basically playing for next season and so I am in the mindset of acquiring draft capital.  If I am going to lose a 2nd, I would like to get the 3rd then as well. Also, I would also be interested in the trade without giving up my 2nd and not acquiring your 3rd.  Let me know.

trades.comments: How about this?
I'll take 1 of those guys
Interested in CIN's new TE1?
Did you wanna flip backup QBs with both backups sharing BYE's w/ our starters?
Could you consider trading AJ Brown? He is on a one year contract and you would have Mike Williams available next year along with a first round pick.
This would give you a starting kicker, cheaper draft picks, injury protection at QB and 4 more players.  You would need 4 more players after this trade.
This trade would get you a 1st this year, a Defense, my 1st for your 2nd next year, and alo some much needed cap space.
